body:
Look out: The Senate Majority is in Serious Trouble!

With momentum swinging hard against the president, Republicans are playing defense on battlefields once thought secure. There are 35 seats at stake in November (two of which, Arizona and one of the Georgia seats, are special elections) and Republicans hold 23 of those seats. Alabama, liberated from the anchor candidacy of Roy Moore, has been expected to flip to any Republican candidate who can spell their own name, in this case former football coach Tommy Tuberville. That means that the Democrats need to flip four seats, assuming Biden wins the presidency.
Can they flip that many Republican seats? The answer increasingly appears to be, “yes.”

Republicans trail significantly in AZ and Colorado, and Maine, Iowa, North Carolina, and Montana are all tossups. Other than Alabama, the only other state where Republicans have a chance to flip a Senate seat is Michigan, but the excellent John James appears anchored by President Trump.

Dick Morris: Trump Can Still Win

Only the Electoral College or the various state legislatures can declare a candidate the winner. To base this decision on network vote totals and projections and to call Biden the president-elect is irresponsible.

The recounts in Arizona, Georgia, and the other states are likely to go heavily for Trump.

Most of the likely errors or invalid votes took place on mailed in ballots. (Machine votes are harder to tamper with). Since Biden won upwards of two-thirds of mail-in votes and absentee ballots, it's likely that most of the discarded mail ballots will be subtracted from Biden’s total.

The networks currently give Trump 214 electorate voters (270 is the victory level).
